title: Extract comment operations from mcp_server.py to comments.py

brief.why: add_comment, list_comments, resolve_comment logic is inline in the MCP tool. comments.py is a stub. Mixing Y.Map operations with MCP tool wiring makes both harder to test and reason about.
brief.what: Move add_comment, list_comments, and resolve_comment from mcp_server.py to comments.py. collect_affected and reanchor already live there — the MCP tools should too.
brief.done: No comment Y.Map operations in mcp_server.py. All comment logic in comments.py. All tests pass.

title: Implement mcp_server.py — FastMCP with Yjs peer connection and MCP tools

brief.why: The MCP server is how Claude connects to the Yjs document. It needs to maintain a persistent WebSocket connection to the ASGI server as a pycrdt Provider, and expose tools that map to document.py operations.
brief.what: 1. AppState/RoomConnection classes for lifecycle management — httpx client, per-room WebSocket+Provider, AsyncExitStack cleanup 2. FastMCP lifespan creates httpx client, lazy room connections on first tool call 3. Four tools: read_doc(room), edit_doc(room, content, mode), load_doc(room, markdown), list_docs() 4. Add /api/rooms HTTP endpoint to asgi_server.py — queries in-memory rooms + SQLite store 5. Integration tests: read_doc returns content, edit_doc appears in second client, replace_section works through MCP, list_docs returns room names
brief.done: mcp_server.py runs via python -m tafelmusik.mcp_server. Integration tests pass: write via MCP → read via separate pycrdt client shows changes. Read via MCP after browser-side write shows content.

steps: AppState/RoomConnection classes for lifecycle management — httpx client, per-room WebSocket+Provider, AsyncExitStack cleanup
FastMCP lifespan creates httpx client, lazy room connections on first tool call
Four tools: read_doc(room), edit_doc(room, content, mode), load_doc(room, markdown), list_docs()
Add /api/rooms HTTP endpoint to asgi_server.py — queries in-memory rooms + SQLite store
Integration tests: read_doc returns content, edit_doc appears in second client, replace_section works through MCP, list_docs returns room names

title: Documents are markdown files on disk, not just CRDT blobs in SQLite

brief.why: Current architecture locks content inside SQLite Yjs updates — can't cat, git diff, or edit with other tools. Current notification model pushes doc-change noise; design calls for comments as the collaboration protocol with drift-aware content delivery.
brief.what: Phase 1 — File Lifecycle (done): 1. docs_dir config, file hydration, flush_doc, directory listing, path validation. Phase 2 — Comment-Driven Notifications (see docs/calute-phase2-brief.md for sequencing and implementation detail): 2. Replace doc-change observer with comment observer — document-body edits = silence, comments = turn signal 3. Drift score per room via state vector snapshots — len(doc.get_update(snapshot)) is native CRDT metric 4. System-push replaces read_doc — three triggers: (a) room connect = full doc, (b) comment + high drift = full doc + comment, (c) idle 30-60s after major surgery + high drift = full doc. Claude never pulls. 5. Act-from-context: Claude-authored doc + low drift + comment = patch from quote, zero content tokens. Phase 3 — Spawn-on-Comment: 6. ASGI webhook when comment arrives with no MCP peer connected, dispatcher spawns Claude Code on hezza.
brief.done: Phase 1: .md files hydrate into CRDT, flush writes back, directory listing works. Phase 2: Claude receives NO doc-change notifications. Comments trigger context-appropriate push (comment-only when drift low, full doc when drift high). read_doc removed from default tools. Drift score visible in inspect_doc. Phase 3: Comment on unattended doc spawns a Claude session on hezza that responds.

title: Observer detects remote edits and produces semantic diffs

brief.why: The MCP server needs to know when Sameer edits, distinguish his edits from Claude's, and describe what changed in terms Claude can act on (section names, not raw CRDT deltas)
brief.what: 1. Add text.observe() 2-param callback to RoomConnection — checks txn.origin, skips 'claude', queues via asyncio.Queue.put_nowait() 2. Add async consumer task started per-room — debounces (2s window), caches previous markdown, diffs to identify changed sections via find_section 3. Produce structured change summary: list of (section_heading, change_type) tuples
brief.done: Unit test: local write with origin=claude produces no queue event. Remote update produces queue event. Debounced consumer produces correct section-level diff.

steps: Add text.observe() 2-param callback to RoomConnection — checks txn.origin, skips 'claude', queues via asyncio.Queue.put_nowait()
Add async consumer task started per-room — debounces (2s window), caches previous markdown, diffs to identify changed sections via find_section
Produce structured change summary: list of (section_heading, change_type) tuples

title: Channel notification reaches Claude Code conversation

brief.why: The observer produces diffs but they need to reach Claude's conversation as channel notifications. This requires capturing the MCP session reference and constructing the right JSONRPCNotification format.
brief.what: 1. Capture ServerSession on first tool call — store in AppState.session 2. Consumer task sends JSONRPCNotification(method='notifications/claude/channel', params={content, meta}) via session._write_stream.send() 3. Update plugin.json channel config and document --dangerously-load-development-channels startup
brief.done: Integration test: browser edit through real server produces channel notification. Plugin config updated. CLAUDE.md documents the startup flag.

steps: Capture ServerSession on first tool call — store in AppState.session
Consumer task sends JSONRPCNotification(method='notifications/claude/channel', params={content, meta}) via session._write_stream.send()
Update plugin.json channel config and document --dangerously-load-development-channels startup

title: File browser UI — docs_dir is ~/Repos, URLs are plain file paths

brief.why: No way to create, name, or switch between documents without typing URLs. With calute, rooms are file paths — but docs_dir is currently a sandboxed subdirectory. Setting it to ~/Repos makes any markdown file on the machine a Tafelmusik document. The URL should be the path, not ?room=path.
brief.what: 1. Change docs_dir default to ~/Repos (or make it configurable, defaulting to ~/Repos) 2. Drop ?room= parameter — URL path IS the file path relative to docs_dir (e.g. tafelmusik:3456/batterie/tafelmusik/docs/calute-design) 3. Document switcher — lists .md files, shows active indicator, navigable tree since paths have directory structure 4. New doc flow — input for path-style name, creates empty room, flush creates the .md file 5. Recently accessed ordering (localStorage) 6. Path validation changes: no sandbox escape check needed, serve anything the user can access
brief.done: Can open any .md file under ~/Repos by navigating to its path. Document switcher shows directory tree. Can create new docs by entering a path. No ?room= in URLs.

steps: Change docs_dir default to ~/Repos (or make it configurable, defaulting to ~/Repos)
Drop ?room= parameter — URL path IS the file path relative to docs_dir (e.g. tafelmusik:3456/batterie/tafelmusik/docs/calute-design)
Document switcher — lists .md files, shows active indicator, navigable tree since paths have directory structure
New doc flow — input for path-style name, creates empty room, flush creates the .md file
Recently accessed ordering (localStorage)
Path validation changes: no sandbox escape check needed, serve anything the user can access

title: Claude is aware of all active rooms, not just ones it's touched

brief.why: Currently MCP observer only fires for rooms where a tool call triggered connect(). Sameer editing a room Claude hasn't visited produces no notification — breaks the bilateral loop for new/unfamiliar rooms.
brief.what: 1. Evaluate options: periodic room-list poll vs ASGI push vs connect-all-on-startup 2. Implement chosen approach 3. Test: browser creates new room + edits, Claude gets notified without prior tool call
brief.done: Channel notification arrives for a room Claude has never read_doc'd

steps: Evaluate options: periodic room-list poll vs ASGI push vs connect-all-on-startup
Implement chosen approach
Test: browser creates new room + edits, Claude gets notified without prior tool call

title: Keepalive detects dead connections in integration tests, not just mocks

brief.why: The keepalive test uses MockChannel — a real server that silently dies (kill -9, network partition) exercises the full path: probe → no response → dead event → reconnection. Without this, keepalive logic is only verified against in-memory behaviour.
brief.what: 1. Write integration test: start server, connect MCP, kill server process, verify keepalive detects death and dead event fires within expected time 2. Verify reconnection works after keepalive-triggered death
brief.done: Integration test passes with a real ASGI server that is killed mid-session. Keepalive fires, dead event set, reconnection succeeds.

steps: Write integration test: start server, connect MCP, kill server process, verify keepalive detects death and dead event fires within expected time
Verify reconnection works after keepalive-triggered death

title: Patch operation in document.py — content-addressed find/replace

brief.why: replace_section stomps authorship and has excessive blast radius for small edits (typos, rewording)
brief.what: 1. Add patch(text, find, replace, author) to document.py 2. Literal text match, fail on 0 or 2+ matches 3. Delete only matched range, insert replacement with author attr 4. Tests: single match, no match error, ambiguous match error, authorship preserved on surrounding text
brief.done: patch() passes all 4 test categories, editing-grammar scenarios 1-3 have a tested path

steps: Add patch(text, find, replace, author) to document.py
Literal text match, fail on 0 or 2+ matches
Delete only matched range, insert replacement with author attr
Tests: single match, no match error, ambiguous match error, authorship preserved on surrounding text

title: Comment UI in CodeMirror — select text, Cmd+M, anchored decorations

brief.why: Frontend defines the Y.Map schema and validates the UX before backend work
brief.what: 1. Define Y.Map 'comments' schema in JS — id, anchor offset, quote, author, body, resolved 2. Cmd+M on selection opens inline comment input 3. Submitted comments write to Y.Map and decorate the quoted text 4. Click decoration to view/resolve comment 5. Rebuild bundle
brief.done: Select text, Cmd+M, type comment, see underlined text with popover showing comment body. Comments persist across page reloads via Yjs sync

steps: Define Y.Map 'comments' schema in JS — id, anchor offset, quote, author, body, resolved
Cmd+M on selection opens inline comment input
Submitted comments write to Y.Map and decorate the quoted text
Click decoration to view/resolve comment
Rebuild bundle

title: Claude reads and edits documents as a Yjs peer via MCP

brief.why: Without MCP tools, Claude can't participate in the collaboration. The MCP server is Claude's hands — read_doc, edit_doc, replace_section, load_doc. This is what makes Claude a peer rather than a bystander.
brief.what: 1. Create mcp_server.py — FastMCP with stdio transport, connects to ASGI server via WebSocket as pycrdt client (HttpxWebsocket + Provider pattern validated in spike) 2. Create document.py — Y.Text operations: read (str(text)), edit (replace/append/replace_section), load (clear + write from markdown). replace_section finds heading by text match, replaces until next heading of equal/higher level 3. Implement tools: read_doc(room), edit_doc(room, content, mode), load_doc(room, markdown), list_docs() 4. Create .claude-plugin/plugin.json with mcpServers config (TAFELMUSIK_URL env var) 5. Create hooks/ensure-tafelmusik.sh — SessionStart hook checks uv, .venv, ASGI server running, handles stale port cleanup 6. Test: read_doc returns markdown matching editor; edit_doc changes appear in browser; integration round-trip both directions
brief.done: mcp__tafelmusik__read_doc() works in a Claude Code session. Edits via MCP appear in browser without refresh. Browser edits readable via MCP.

done_note: All 6 actions complete. Code block fix in find_section was a bonus hardening pass.

title: Property-based tests catch replace_section edge cases humans miss

brief.why: replace_section has combinatorial edge cases — heading levels, whitespace patterns, empty sections, headings at document boundaries. Point tests cover known cases; Hypothesis generates the unknown ones. Without it, a future edit to the algorithm could silently break a pattern nobody tested.
brief.what: 1. Add hypothesis to dev deps 2. Write strategies for generating markdown docs with headings 3. Properties: after replace, heading exists in result; replace then read contains new content; section boundaries never overlap; idempotent replace gives same result 4. Run and fix any failures found
brief.done: hypothesis in dev deps. Property tests in document_test.py pass. At least 3 invariant properties tested across random markdown inputs.

steps: Add hypothesis to dev deps
Write strategies for generating markdown docs with headings
Properties: after replace, heading exists in result; replace then read contains new content; section boundaries never overlap; idempotent replace gives same result
Run and fix any failures found

title: Frontend tests for comments UI

brief.why: State machine, decorations, sort, compose lifecycle all untested. Next bug will be debugged by staring at code instead of running tests.
brief.what: Test the two-mode state machine transitions, decoration building from Y.Map data, overlap-based sort correctness, compose card show/hide/position lifecycle
brief.done: Key comment UI behaviors have automated tests that catch regressions

title: ASGI server owns its WebSocket room management instead of using pycrdt WebsocketServer/YRoom

brief.why: The ASGI server depends on pycrdt.websocket's WebsocketServer and YRoom — same coupling risk we just eliminated on the MCP client side. WebsocketServer's internals (room lifecycle, client tracking, broadcast) are a black box. Owning this removes version-pinning anxiety for the server process that runs 24/7.
brief.what: 1. Audit WebsocketServer and YRoom usage in asgi_server.py — identify which features we actually use 2. Implement standalone room management using public pycrdt APIs (Doc, Y.Text, sync functions) 3. Replace WebsocketServer/YRoom imports 4. All tests pass
brief.done: No import of pycrdt.websocket in asgi_server.py. Room management, client tracking, and persistence all use public pycrdt APIs. All tests pass.

steps: Audit WebsocketServer and YRoom usage in asgi_server.py — identify which features we actually use
Implement standalone room management using public pycrdt APIs (Doc, Y.Text, sync functions)
Replace WebsocketServer/YRoom imports
All tests pass

title: Claude receives push notifications when Sameer edits

brief.why: Without change notification, Claude only discovers edits when explicitly asked to read. Channels break the turn-taking model — Claude knows Sameer reordered section 3 without being told. This is what makes the collaboration feel live rather than relay-based.
brief.what: 1. Add text.observe() with 2-param callback to RoomConnection — synchronous callback checks txn.origin, skips "claude", queues remote changes via asyncio.Queue.put_nowait() 2. Add async consumer task: debounces queued changes (2s window), produces semantic diff (cache previous markdown, parse headings via find_section to identify changed section, structured change summary) 3. Send channel notification via captured session — capture ServerSession on first tool call into AppState, consumer sends JSONRPCNotification(method="notifications/claude/channel") through session._write_stream 4. Update plugin.json and startup docs for --dangerously-load-development-channels 5. Test: browser edit triggers notification in Claude session, Claude edit produces no self-notification, rapid edits debounced
brief.done: Claude Code session with channels enabled receives notification when browser edits the doc. Claude edits via MCP produce no self-notification. Rapid edits debounced into single notification.

done_note: All 3 actions complete: observer+diffs, channel notifications, room awareness via polling. 83 tests pass.

title: Documents export cleanly to Google Docs with images intact

brief.why: Google Docs is the delivery format — when documents need to reach other humans (stakeholders, team members), they go to Docs. Without export, every finished document requires manual copy-paste. Images are the hard part: Google's API fetches images server-side and can't reach Tailscale URLs.
brief.what: 1. Add export_to_docs(room, title) MCP tool — flushes first (ensures .md is current), then reads the .md file, converts markdown to Google Docs API calls via mise do(operation='create', source=...) 2. Handle images: for each image URL in the markdown, use mise's upload-share-insert-revoke pattern (temporary public URL for Google's servers to fetch) 3. Test: export document with headings, tables, lists, code blocks — all formatting survives. Export document with images — images appear in Google Doc
brief.done: export_to_docs('Q1 Report') flushes to .md then creates a Google Doc with correct formatting. Headings, tables, lists, bold/italic, code blocks intact. Images present via mise upload-share-insert-revoke pattern.

title: Wire patch mode into edit_doc + guard h1 replace_section

brief.why: patch() needs to be callable via MCP tool, and h1 replace_section silently destroys documents
brief.what: 1. Add mode=patch to edit_doc with find+replace params 2. Refuse replace_section on h1 headings with clear error message 3. Tests for both paths
brief.done: edit_doc(mode='patch') works end-to-end, h1 replace_section returns error not destruction

steps: Add mode=patch to edit_doc with find+replace params
Refuse replace_section on h1 headings with clear error message
Tests for both paths

title: File-backed rooms: hydrate from .md, flush to .md, directory listing

brief.why: Phase 1 of calute — the core file lifecycle that makes documents real files on disk
brief.what: 1. Add docs_dir config to ASGI server create_app() 2. Hydrate Y.Text from .md file on room open (file → SQLite fallback → empty) 3. New flush_doc MCP tool: read Y.Text, write .md, wipe comments, git commit 4. Directory listing in list_rooms merges memory + SQLite + .md files 5. Integration tests: hydrate, flush, round-trip, directory listing
brief.done: Existing .md file opens in Tafelmusik with content in Y.Text. flush_doc writes Y.Text to .md and wipes comments. list_rooms shows .md files from docs_dir. New doc created via browser flush creates new .md file with parent dirs. All existing tests still pass.

steps: Add docs_dir config to ASGI server create_app()
Hydrate Y.Text from .md file on room open (file → SQLite fallback → empty)
New flush_doc MCP tool: read Y.Text, write .md, wipe comments, git commit
Directory listing in list_rooms merges memory + SQLite + .md files
Integration tests: hydrate, flush, round-trip, directory listing

title: Editor polish: formatting shortcuts, line numbers, connection stability

brief.why: Cmd+B/I don't work, line numbers are on the wrong side, WebSocket blips during comment entry. Small affordances that make writing feel right and connection feel solid.
brief.what: 1. Keyboard shortcuts for Cmd+B bold, Cmd+I italic 2. Line number gutter on right edge 3. Fix connection blips during comment compose 4. Browser handles server restart without CRDT duplication
brief.done: Cmd+B/I wrap selections. Line numbers on right. No connection drops during comment entry. Server restart doesn't duplicate content.

title: Editor UI is clean and minimal — no clutter, no jank

brief.why: Current UI has branding, misplaced indicators, ugly heading rendering, and a dead split pane. Cleaning up first makes room for comments pane and avoids building on top of jank.
brief.what: 1. Remove Tafelmusik branding top left 2. Move Connected lozenge to top right 3. Strip split/preview pane 4. Heading rendering: bold weight not underlines
brief.done: Editor shows only the document, connection indicator top right, headings render with bold weight

done_note: Stripped branding, moved lozenge top-right, removed split/preview pane and marked dep, added CM6 heading bold styles

title: MCP server owns its sync protocol instead of subclassing Provider

brief.why: SyncAwareProvider uses 5 private APIs from pycrdt Provider (validated against 0.12.50). Any pycrdt update risks silent breakage. Owning the ~100-line protocol removes version-pinning anxiety and the runtime assertion hack.
brief.what: 1. Implement standalone sync handler using public pycrdt functions (create_sync_message, handle_sync_message, Doc.observe) 2. Replace SyncAwareProvider with the new handler in _provider_task 3. Remove Provider import, runtime assertion, and version coupling 4. Verify all 37 tests pass
brief.done: No import of pycrdt.websocket.yroom.Provider anywhere in mcp_server.py. All tests pass. Sync, reconnection, and timeout behaviour unchanged.

steps: Implement standalone sync handler using public pycrdt functions (create_sync_message, handle_sync_message, Doc.observe)
Replace SyncAwareProvider with the new handler in _provider_task
Remove Provider import, runtime assertion, and version coupling
Verify all 37 tests pass

title: Connection blips during comment entry — WebSocket drops momentarily

brief.why: When entering a comment in the compose card, the WebSocket connection appears to blip — likely the browser briefly disconnects or the sync loop hiccups. Visible as a flash or stutter. Unclear if it is the compose card stealing focus, a Y.Map transaction causing a sync burst, or something else.
brief.what: 1. Reproduce: open editor, select text, enter comment compose, type — watch for connection indicator or console errors 2. Check if the compose card's CM6 instance interferes with the main editor's WebSocket (separate Doc? shared Doc?) 3. Check if Y.Map comment insertion triggers a sync message that causes the provider to reconnect 4. Fix root cause — may be debouncer interaction, may be focus-related WebSocket timeout, may be transaction size
brief.done: Can enter and submit a comment without any visible connection drop, WebSocket reconnection, or content flicker.

title: Path validation follows symlinks — symlink inside docs_dir can escape

brief.why: A symlink inside docs_dir pointing outside it bypasses _safe_doc_path since Path.resolve() follows symlinks before checking is_relative_to. Could read/write arbitrary files.
brief.what: 1. Add symlink test: create symlink in docs_dir pointing outside, verify hydration is rejected 2. Check resolved path AND that no component is a symlink escaping docs_dir, or use os.path.realpath on docs_dir itself
brief.done: Symlink pointing outside docs_dir is rejected by _safe_doc_path. Test proves it.

done_note: Won't fix — theoretical risk only. docs_dir is on your own machine, you'd have to deliberately create a malicious symlink. With dibuca moving docs_dir to ~/Repos, path validation changes entirely anyway.

title: Non-transitive sort comparator — overlap-based comment ordering is unstable at scale

brief.why: JS sort with non-transitive comparator produces different card orderings across page reloads. Silent confusion when 20+ comments on adjacent text.
brief.what: Replace overlap-based sort with a grouping pass: cluster overlapping ranges, sort clusters by min(from), sort within clusters by created. Or compute transitive closure of overlaps.
brief.done: Comment card ordering is deterministic regardless of comment count or overlap pattern

title: Re-anchor comments after replace_section

brief.why: Claude's primary editing mode destroys RelativePosition anchors. Comments vanish silently — the most likely way comments break in practice.
brief.what: Before replace_section: collect comments in affected range. After: search for quote text in new content, re-create anchorStart/anchorEnd. Orphan comments whose quote was deleted entirely. Notify via list_comments.
brief.done: replace_section on a section with comments preserves those whose quote text survived. Orphaned comments are flagged in list_comments.

steps: comments.py — collect_affected + reanchor functions
Integrate into edit_doc — wrap replace_section/replace_all with reanchor
list_comments — show orphaned status
Browser UI — grey out orphaned comments, float to bottom
Tests in comments_test.py
End-to-end verify

title: Comments anchored to text flow bidirectionally between Sameer and Claude

brief.why: Comments are the collaboration mechanism — 'expand this section', 'make this punchier', 'I moved this'. Without anchored comments, feedback happens in chat, disconnected from the text. This is what makes Tafelmusik more than a shared text buffer.
brief.what: 1. Create comments.py — Y.Map 'comments' in Y.Doc, keyed by comment ID. Each comment: {anchor: StickyIndex, quote: str, author, body, resolved}. Point+quote architecture: single anchor provides location, stored quote provides resilience and re-anchoring after replace_section. Use StickyIndex.new(text, idx, Assoc.AFTER) and encode()/decode() for storage 2. Add MCP tools to mcp_server.py: add_comment(quote, body) — find quote text, create StickyIndex at match position, store in Y.Map. list_comments() — return all comments with current anchor positions and quote text. resolve_comment(id) — mark resolved 3. Add re-anchoring to document.py replace_section: before replacing, collect comments in affected range; after replacing, search for quote text and re-anchor; orphan comments whose quote was deleted 4. Add CodeMirror comment decorations to cm-entry.js: read Y.Map, resolve StickyIndex to offset, search forward for quote text, underline match. Select text + Cmd+M opens comment input 5. Rebuild JS bundle
brief.done: Claude adds comment via add_comment, Sameer sees underlined text with popover. Sameer adds comment via Cmd+M, list_comments returns it. Comments survive concurrent edits. Replace_section re-anchors comments whose quote text survived.

done_note: All three actions complete (fetaza, raleni, mafuze). Done criteria met: Claude adds comments via MCP, Sameer via Cmd+M, comments survive concurrent edits, replace_section re-anchors.

title: Validate all editing-grammar scenarios have safe paths

brief.why: The editing grammar is the acceptance criteria — scenarios from real use must each map to a tested operation
brief.what: 1. Map each scenario in editing-grammar.md to a concrete tool call 2. Write integration tests proving each scenario 3. Update editing-grammar.md with the solution for each
brief.done: Every scenario in editing-grammar.md has a 'Solution' subsection and a passing test

steps: Map each scenario in editing-grammar.md to a concrete tool call
Write integration tests proving each scenario
Update editing-grammar.md with the solution for each

title: Remove read_doc from default tool surface — system pushes, Claude never pulls

brief.why: Design principle from calute session: 'the efficient path is the only path.' If read_doc exists, Claude will use it defensively before every edit (the harness trains this anxiety). With comment-driven notifications + drift-aware push, Claude gets content when the system decides it is needed. Three modes: (1) act-from-context — Claude wrote the doc, comment quote + instruction is enough, near-zero tokens. (2) Incremental — section diffs keep model current via notifications. (3) Full resync — drift too high, system pushes whole doc. No mode requires Claude to pull.
brief.what: 1. Remove read_doc from @mcp.tool() registration (keep the function for internal use if needed by other tools) 2. Verify all three content delivery paths work without read_doc: room-connect push, comment+high-drift push, idle-resync push 3. Update CLAUDE.md and tool documentation — explain the push model so future Claudes don't wonder where read_doc went 4. Consider: keep read_doc as a gated escape hatch? e.g. only available after N failed patches suggesting stale model. Or remove entirely and let the drift system handle it.
brief.done: read_doc is not in the MCP tool list. Claude receives content exclusively via push. A full session of comment-reply-edit workflow completes without Claude ever requesting document content.

steps: Remove read_doc from @mcp.tool() registration (keep the function for internal use if needed by other tools)
Verify all three content delivery paths work without read_doc: room-connect push, comment+high-drift push, idle-resync push
Update CLAUDE.md and tool documentation — explain the push model so future Claudes don't wonder where read_doc went
Consider: keep read_doc as a gated escape hatch? e.g. only available after N failed patches suggesting stale model. Or remove entirely and let the drift system handle it.

title: Images drag-and-drop into the editor and persist on hezza

brief.why: Documents without images are incomplete. Sameer needs to paste screenshots, diagrams, photos into the editor without leaving the browser. Images must persist across restarts and be accessible over Tailscale for Google Docs export. Images must NOT enter Claude's conversation context — file deposit pattern.
brief.what: 1. Add POST /upload route to asgi_server.py — multipart, saves to data/uploads/<uuid>.<ext>, MIME type validation for common image formats 2. Create uploads.py — upload handling logic, UUID filename generation, MIME type mapping 3. Add GET /uploads/* static file serving to asgi_server.py 4. Add drop/paste handler to cm-entry.js — intercept file drops and clipboard pastes, upload via fetch, insert \![filename](url) at cursor position 5. Rebuild JS bundle after editor changes 6. Test: drag image into editor, markdown image reference inserted, image accessible via URL from Mac, image persists across server restart, non-image file handled gracefully
brief.done: Image visible in preview pane after drag-and-drop upload. URL accessible from Mac via Tailscale. Images persist across server restarts.

title: Code changes auto-deploy to the running ASGI server on push

brief.why: Currently requires manual systemctl --user restart tafelmusik after every code change on hezza. During rapid iteration this is friction that compounds — forgotten restarts mean stale server, confusing debugging, wasted time.
brief.what: 1. Create a post-push git hook or systemd path unit that watches the repo 2. Restart tafelmusik.service on relevant file changes (src/ only, not tests or docs) 3. Verify restart is clean (no orphan connections, SQLite store intact)
brief.done: Push to hezza triggers automatic restart of tafelmusik.service. Verified: push code change, confirm server picks it up without manual intervention.

steps: Create a post-push git hook or systemd path unit that watches the repo
Restart tafelmusik.service on relevant file changes (src/ only, not tests or docs)
Verify restart is clean (no orphan connections, SQLite store intact)

title: Room lifecycle test covers cleanup-and-restore round-trip

brief.why: Room cleanup (remove_if_empty) and restore (_restore_ydoc) are tested separately but never together. A bug where cleanup corrupts store state or restore misses updates would pass all existing tests.
brief.what: 1. Test: connect → write → disconnect → verify room cleaned up from manager → reconnect → verify data persisted from SQLite 2. Test: two clients, one disconnects (room stays), second disconnects (room cleaned up)
brief.done: Two tests exercising the full create→cleanup→restore path. Both pass.

steps: Test: connect → write → disconnect → verify room cleaned up from manager → reconnect → verify data persisted from SQLite
Test: two clients, one disconnects (room stays), second disconnects (room cleaned up)

title: Drift score tracking and context-aware content push

brief.why: Claude's mental model of a doc drifts as Sameer edits. Small edits (typo fixes) = model still accurate, no push needed. Major surgery (restructure, rewrite) = model stale, needs resync. The CRDT already has this metric natively: doc.get_update(old_state_vector) returns binary diff, byte size = drift. Three push triggers from design conversation: (a) comment arrives + high drift = push full doc alongside comment, (b) idle 30-60s after major surgery (no comment) + high drift = push full resync, (c) room connect = always push full doc. Low drift + comment = push comment only, Claude acts from context.
brief.what: 1. Store state vector snapshot per room in MCP server (snapshot after each full push) 2. On each remote edit event, compute drift = len(doc.get_update(last_snapshot)) 3. On comment notification: if drift > THRESHOLD, include full doc content in the channel notification alongside comment. If drift <= THRESHOLD, send comment only. 4. Add idle-after-surgery timer: if drift > THRESHOLD and no edits for 30-60s, push full doc as resync (no comment needed). 5. On room connect (poller joins room), push full doc content as initial context. 6. Expose drift score in inspect_doc for debugging. 7. The THRESHOLD is a tuning parameter — start conservative (e.g. 1KB of update bytes), adjust based on real usage. Document in CLAUDE.md.
brief.done: inspect_doc shows drift score. Comment on a doc Claude wrote (low drift) arrives as comment-only notification. Comment on a doc Sameer heavily edited (high drift) arrives with full doc content. Idle after major surgery triggers automatic resync push.

title: File-backed rooms need eviction policy for long-running servers

brief.why: File-backed rooms stay in memory forever after last client disconnects. On a long-running server with many .md files, each room touched once stays with its Doc, store, and background task indefinitely. But if we're evicting, we should flush first — the eviction IS a flush.
brief.what: 1. Add idle timeout for file-backed rooms (e.g. 30 min since last client disconnect) 2. On eviction: flush Y.Text to .md file, then stop room cleanly (cancel background task, close store) 3. Next connection re-hydrates from the now-current .md file 4. Timeout must be longer than typical WebSocket reconnection cycles to avoid the CRDT duplication bug 5. Log eviction + flush so it is visible in journalctl
brief.done: File-backed room with no clients for 30+ minutes is flushed to .md and evicted from memory. Reconnection after eviction re-hydrates cleanly from the flushed file. Flush is visible in logs.

title: Claude can add comments via MCP that appear in the browser

brief.why: Schema compatibility between pycrdt and Yjs is unverified — the whole bidirectional promise depends on it
brief.what: 1. Add add_comment MCP tool to mcp_server.py 2. Verify anchor serialization is compatible (StickyIndex vs RelativePosition) 3. Test round-trip: Claude adds comment, browser renders it
brief.done: Claude runs add_comment, browser shows the comment card with author=claude and yellow underline on the quoted text

steps: Add add_comment MCP tool to mcp_server.py
Verify anchor serialization is compatible (StickyIndex vs RelativePosition)
Test round-trip: Claude adds comment, browser renders it

title: Keyboard shortcuts for markdown formatting — Cmd+B bold, Cmd+I italic

brief.why: Writing markdown by hand is friction. Cmd+B/I are muscle memory from every other editor. Currently nothing happens when you press them.
brief.what: 1. Add CodeMirror keymap extension for Cmd+B — wraps selection in ** or inserts **** with cursor between 2. Add Cmd+I — wraps selection in * or inserts ** with cursor between 3. Consider Cmd+U for underline (not standard markdown but common expectation) 4. Rebuild JS bundle after changes
brief.done: Cmd+B wraps selected text in ** (or inserts bold markers). Cmd+I wraps in *. Works on both Mac and via Ctrl on other platforms.

title: Tune DRIFT_THRESHOLD from production logs

brief.why: 1024B is a guess — may trigger unnecessary resyncs on casual edits, wasting tokens. journalctl now logs drift scores on every comment and idle timer fire.
brief.what: Review drift score distribution in journalctl on hezza after real usage sessions. Adjust DRIFT_THRESHOLD (and possibly IDLE_TIMEOUT) based on observed patterns. Update CLAUDE.md with rationale.
brief.done: DRIFT_THRESHOLD value justified by observed data, not guesswork.

title: Implement document.py — Y.Text operations with section parsing

brief.why: Claude's primary editing mode is replace_section — finding a heading and replacing content until the next heading of equal/higher level. This needs to work correctly on Y.Text CRDT types, not plain strings, so edits propagate to all peers.
brief.what: 1. read(text) returns str(text) 2. replace_all(text, content) clears and writes via slice assignment 3. find_section(content, heading) returns (start, end) character indices 4. replace_section(text, new_content) finds heading, replaces section, or appends if not found 5. Unit tests covering: section finding, replacement, append-when-missing, edge cases (last section, nested headings, empty doc)
brief.done: document.py has read, replace_all, replace_section. document_test.py passes with pytest. All operations work on pycrdt.Text objects.

steps: read(text) returns str(text)
replace_all(text, content) clears and writes via slice assignment
find_section(content, heading) returns (start, end) character indices
replace_section(text, new_content) finds heading, replaces section, or appends if not found
Unit tests covering: section finding, replacement, append-when-missing, edge cases (last section, nested headings, empty doc)

title: Spawn Claude on hezza when comment arrives with no MCP peer

brief.why: Phase 3: if Sameer comments on a doc and no Claude session is connected, the comment goes unanswered. The ASGI server knows whether an MCP peer is connected (room has clients). A webhook + dispatcher on hezza can spawn a Claude Code session with the right cwd and channel flag, so Sameer can comment-and-walk-away.
brief.what: 1. ASGI server: detect comment added to Y.Map when no MCP-identified peer is in the room 2. POST to a local webhook endpoint on hezza with room name + comment content 3. Dispatcher script spawns claude --dangerously-load-development-channels server:tafelmusik in the correct cwd 4. Consider integration with Aboyeur for multi-repo orchestration 5. Test: add comment to doc with no active Claude session, verify Claude session spawns and responds
brief.done: Comment on an unattended document triggers a Claude Code session on hezza that connects to the room, reads the comment, and responds within 60 seconds.

title: Strip editor to essentials — branding, lozenge, split pane, heading style

brief.why: Four discrete UI fixes all in index.html/cm-entry.js — small enough for one pass
brief.what: 1. Remove Tafelmusik branding top left 2. Move Connected lozenge to top right 3. Strip split/preview pane entirely 4. Heading rendering: bold weight not underlines
brief.done: Editor shows only document area and connection lozenge top right. No branding, no split pane, headings are bold not underlined

steps: Remove Tafelmusik branding top left
Move Connected lozenge to top right
Strip split/preview pane entirely
Heading rendering: bold weight not underlines

title: Comment observer replaces doc-change observer in MCP server

brief.why: Current observer fires on every remote text mutation — noisy, sends doc-change notifications Claude shouldn't act on. Design says: document-body edits = Sameer working = silence. Comments = turn signal = notification. The observer needs to watch Y.Map 'comments' for new/changed entries, not Y.Text 'content' for mutations.
brief.what: 1. Add Y.Map 'comments' observer alongside or replacing the Y.Text observer in mcp_server.py 2. Comment observer detects new comment entries (compare keys before/after) and fires notification with quote + body + author 3. Remove or gate the current doc-change section-diff notification — only fire if drift score exceeds threshold (see next action) 4. Keep the debouncer but tune it for comments (discrete events, shorter debounce than doc edits) 5. Test: Sameer types in browser = no notification. Sameer adds comment = notification with quote and body.
brief.done: Claude receives zero notifications from Sameer typing in the document body. Claude receives a notification within 2s of Sameer adding a comment containing the comment quote, body, and author.

title: Browser-based collaborative editor serves Y.Text documents

brief.why: Foundation for everything else — without the ASGI server holding a Y.Doc and serving CodeMirror, no other unit can function. Sameer needs to edit markdown in a browser; Claude needs a WebSocket endpoint to connect to.
brief.what: 1. Create asgi_server.py — pycrdt-websocket ASGIServer + starlette routes, SQLiteYStore persistence, port 3456 2. Create public/index.html + cm-entry.js — CodeMirror 6 with y-codemirror.next binding, split/preview/edit tabs, markdown preview with marked, table/code/blockquote CSS 3. Bundle JS with esbuild (npx esbuild public/cm-entry.js --bundle --outfile=public/editor.js --minify) 4. Create pyproject.toml with all deps, CLAUDE.md, .gitignore 5. Verify: two browser tabs edit simultaneously and sync; content persists across server restart; server restarts while browser connected — reconnects cleanly
brief.done: Editor loads from Mac via Tailscale IP. Two tabs sync. Content survives server restart. Server restart with connected browser reconnects cleanly.

done_note: All done criteria met: editor loads at :3456, two tabs sync (test_two_clients_sync), content persists across restart (test_persistence_across_restart), browser reconnects after restart. ASGI server owns room management with zero pycrdt.websocket dependency. Auto-deploy via systemd timer + post-merge hook.

title: Line number gutter on right edge of editor

brief.why: Line numbers are on the left (CodeMirror default). For a writing-focused editor they belong on the right — less visual weight competing with the text, more like a page margin.
brief.what: 1. Configure CodeMirror gutter placement to right side 2. May need CSS override if CM6 doesn't support gutter position natively 3. Rebuild JS bundle
brief.done: Line numbers appear on the right edge of the editor pane, not the left.

title: Wire ASGI server + CodeMirror editor with live sync

brief.why: Can't test or build anything until the server holds a Y.Doc and serves an editor that syncs via WebSocket
brief.what: 1. Create asgi_server.py — ASGIServer + Starlette routes + SQLiteYStore 2. Create cm-entry.js from cm-entry2.js prototype + index.html 3. Bundle with esbuild 4. Verify: two tabs sync, persistence survives restart, reconnect after server restart
brief.done: Editor loads at :3456 from browser, two tabs sync edits, content persists across server restart, browser reconnects after server restart

steps: Create asgi_server.py — ASGIServer + Starlette routes + SQLiteYStore
Create cm-entry.js from cm-entry2.js prototype + index.html
Bundle with esbuild
Verify: two tabs sync, persistence survives restart, reconnect after server restart

title: End-to-end test for channel notifications over real MCP transport

brief.why: All notification tests use MockSession (list.append). Phase 2's entire comment-as-turn-signal architecture has never been verified over a real MCP stdio transport — we discovered this when wiveje's session capture worked in tests but notifications never reached a live CC session. Without this test, every notification feature ships untested in the real environment.
brief.what: 1. Write a test that starts the tafelmusik MCP server with real stdio transport (not MockSession) 2. Connect a real MCP client, complete the initialize handshake 3. Trigger a comment notification (browser peer adds comment to a room) 4. Assert the client receives the notifications/claude/channel message 5. If step 4 is blocked by client API limitations, at minimum verify the message appears on the raw write stream
brief.done: A pytest test exercises the full path: browser comment → Y.Map observer → comment consumer → ServerSession.send_message() → MCP stdio transport → client receives notification. No MockSession anywhere in the chain.
brief.how: Run a real FastMCP server with stdio transport in-process (or subprocess), send a channel notification via ServerSession.send_message(), and verify the client receives it. The test needs to exercise the actual MCP protocol stack — not MockSession. Check mcp.client for a Python client that can connect to a server and receive notifications. If no client-side notification listener exists, the test can at least verify send_message doesn't throw and the message appears on the write stream.

done_note: Diagnosis via debug logging over real MCP transport proved the pipeline works correctly. The e2e test is moot — the break is CC-side (channel notification delivery bug). No test we write can validate CC's notification injection.

title: Browser discards stale Y.Doc on server restart to prevent CRDT duplication

brief.why: Every deploy restarts the server, re-hydrates from file with new CRDT ops. Browser reconnects with old ops, merge doubles content. Visible as tripling content while idle.
brief.what: 1. Investigate y-websocket WebsocketProvider reconnection behavior 2. Either destroy/recreate Y.Doc on reconnect, or add generation counter so browser detects server reset 3. Test across deploy cycle
brief.done: Browser shows correct single-copy content after server restart without manual refresh

title: MCP session captured at initialization, not first tool call

brief.why: When Sameer opens a NEW doc in the browser and comments, Claude hears nothing — no tool call has happened yet so the MCP session isn't captured. For existing docs Claude would have called a tool to create/edit them, capturing the session. With Phase 2's comment-as-turn-signal model this matters more: if the first interaction is always a comment, the session must be ready before any tool call.
brief.what: 1. Narrow the problem: only affects new docs opened directly in browser without Claude involvement 2. Debug why _handle_message monkey-patch broke tool registration (add logging, test in real CC session) 3. Implement working session capture on InitializedNotification or equivalent early hook 4. Verify: open new doc in browser, add comment, Claude receives notification without any prior tool call
brief.done: Fresh session receives channel notification from a browser comment on a brand-new doc without Claude calling any tool first. Verified end-to-end in a real CC session with --dangerously-load-development-channels.

done_note: Session capture implemented and tested (45/45). End-to-end verification blocked by a pre-existing notification pipeline issue — comments sync to Y.Map but notifications don't reach the receiving CC session. Filed separate investigation.

title: Debug notification pipeline: comments sync but notifications don't reach CC

brief.why: Comments are visible in Y.Map (list_comments works) but channel notifications never arrive in the receiving CC session. Discovered during tfm-wiveje verification — a Claude comment added from one session doesn't trigger a notification in another session with --dangerously-load-development-channels. This blocks all comment-driven collaboration (Phase 2's core value).
brief.what: 1. Add diagnostic logging to _on_comments_change observer and _comment_consumer in mcp_server.py 2. Verify observer fires on wire-synced Y.Map changes in production (works in tests) 3. Verify _send_channel_notification succeeds (check if ServerSession.send_message raises) 4. Verify CC receives and displays channel notifications (check --dangerously-load-development-channels behavior) 5. Fix the broken link in the chain
brief.done: A comment added in the browser (or from another CC session) triggers a visible notification in the receiving CC session without any prior tool call.

done_note: Diagnosed: CC-side bug, not ours. Notifications written to stdio correctly but CC drops them in most REPL states. Known regression v2.1.86, multiple open issues (#36975, #37139, #40237, #36477). Research preview, no fix as of v2.1.88.

title: Claude edits surgically without collateral damage

brief.why: Current replace_section is a sledgehammer — h1 swallows docs, typo fixes stomp authorship, concurrent edits collide. Every real editing session will hit these.
brief.what: 1. Add patch mode (content-addressed find/replace) to edit_doc 2. Guard replace_section against h1-replaces-all 3. Validate against editing-grammar.md scenarios as they accumulate
brief.done: All scenarios in docs/editing-grammar.md have a safe, tested editing path

done_note: patch mode implemented, h1 guard added, all 5 editing-grammar scenarios have tested paths. 82 tests pass.
